tasks.title: Migration: add expires_at column to links
tasks.dod: migrate() adds expires_at guarded by PRAGMA table_info (ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN is not idempotent in SQLite); the base CREATE TABLE is untouched; pre-migration rows survive with expires_at NULL and still resolve

tasks.title: Domain: resolve default TTL + compute expired state
tasks.dod: BLOCKED on spec §8 (default TTL) — ask the human first. Then: createLink writes expires_at from a given or default ttl; isExpired(link, now) is correct at the boundary (now >= expires_at) and treats null as non-expiring; resolveLink and listLinks return `expired` and never increment clicks on an expired link

tasks.title: App: refuse expired follows as gone; accept a lifetime on create
tasks.dod: GET /:code returns 410 { error: 'gone' } when expired and 302 when valid, with no click counted on the 410; POST /api/shorten accepts a positive-integer ttl_days and rejects anything else with 400 { error: 'invalid ttl' }

tasks.title: Frontend: expired/active badge in links table
tasks.dod: each listed link shows an expired/active badge read verbatim from the server's `expired` field; no expiry arithmetic in src/public/; proven by tests/e2e/expiry.spec.js

tasks.title: Tests: unit + integration for AC-01..05
tasks.dod: npm run test:fast green; the boundary is asserted from both sides, the legacy expires_at NULL row is exercised, an expired follow provably counts no click, and no test sleeps or touches the real process.env
